name: Model Cities Of St Paul, Inc.
description_short: African American woman-led nonprofit in Saint Paul offering free financial education, housing assistance, and community development services since 1967.
description_long:
Model Cities of St Paul, Inc. is an established community development and human services nonprofit that has been serving marginalized and underserved communities since 1967, with a specific focus on the economic stability and vibrancy of Black Minnesotans in Saint Paul's Frogtown neighborhood. The organization operates as both a community developer and human service provider, delivering interconnected programs designed to support individuals, families, and business owners through a continuum of services. Their approach emphasizes respect, empathy, and human-centered care, guided by their core belief that communities thrive when people thrive.

Model Cities provides a comprehensive range of free and low-cost services including emergency overnight shelter, supportive housing, affordable rental resources, financial education workshops, homebuyer education, pre-purchase counseling, and business support through their BROWNstone Commercial initiative. They served 3,397 individuals agency-wide in 2024, with 729 individuals receiving homeownership education and financial coaching services, and 2,465 unsheltered adults accessing their Safe Space Shelter. Their financial education offerings span money management classes, the HomeStretch workshop program, and one-on-one financial coaching to help participants build pathways toward wealth creation and stability.

What distinguishes Model Cities is their integrated service delivery model that combines shelter, housing, financial literacy, business development, and real estate development under one mission-driven organization. As an African American woman-led nonprofit with over 55 years of community presence, they bring authentic perspective and deep connection to the communities they serve. Their work is grounded in understanding local challenges from multiple angles and addressing systemic barriers to economic stability for underserved populations.

Model Cities is best suited for individuals and families in Saint Paul seeking free financial education, emergency shelter, supportive housing, or business development support. The organization's strength lies in their comprehensive, holistic approach rather than specialized expertise in any single area. Those seeking highly specialized credit counseling or advanced financial services may need to supplement Model Cities' offerings with additional resources, though their services are appropriate for foundational financial stability and housing needs.
pros: Free financial education workshops covering money management, homebuyer education, and pre-purchase counseling with no explicit fees mentioned
Emergency overnight shelter (Safe Space Shelter) accessible to unsheltered adults with wraparound services
Integrated supportive housing programs providing pathways out of homelessness with ongoing support
One-on-one financial coaching and advising for homeownership preparation and wealth-building strategies
African American woman-led organization with 55+ years of community trust and authentic cultural connection
Serves 3,397+ individuals annually across multiple interconnected programs, demonstrating proven community impact
BROWNstone Commercial provides affordable business space and development coaching for entrepreneurs
cons: Geographically focused on Saint Paul's Frogtown neighborhood, limiting accessibility for those outside the service area
Website does not clearly specify whether financial counseling requires HUD or NFCC certification or provide credential details
Limited information on specific financial education curriculum standards, duration of programs, or structured outcomes measurement
Business support and real estate development services may be less developed than shelter and housing offerings
No indication of whether they offer direct debt counseling, credit dispute services, or credit monitoring tools
best_for: Individuals experiencing homelessness or housing instability in Saint Paul seeking emergency shelter and supportive services
First-time homebuyers and renters in the Saint Paul area needing free financial education and pre-purchase counseling
Entrepreneurs and small business owners seeking affordable commercial space and business development coaching
Families seeking holistic support combining financial literacy, housing assistance, and community connection
diagnosis: Model Cities is best for Saint Paul residents seeking free, culturally-grounded financial education, emergency housing assistance, or business support within a holistic, community-centered nonprofit environment. Primary caveat: their services are geographically limited to Saint Paul (primarily Frogtown) and their financial counseling offerings, while comprehensive, are not explicitly positioned as HUD-approved or NFCC-certified credit counseling, so those needing specialized credit repair or debt counseling should verify service scope.
services: Emergency overnight shelter through Safe Space Shelter
Supportive housing programs for individuals transitioning out of homelessness
Affordable rental housing resources and assistance
Financial education workshops and money management classes
BROWNstone Commercial affordable business space leasing
Business coaching, counseling, and training services
Real estate development and community property management
Volunteer opportunities and community engagement programs
Room rental facilities for community events
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 839 University Ave W, Saint Paul, MN 55104
